title: Register application/bolyra-proof+cbor media type and define proof envelope format

artifacts.description: Canonical specification for the proof envelope format: CDDL schema, field semantics, COSE structure alignment, versioning policy, and media type registration rationale per RFC 6838.

Machine-readable CDDL schema defining the top-level ProofEnvelope map, ProofType enum, PublicSignals array, Groth16Proof struct (a/b/c points as bstr), optional DelegationChain array, and protocolVersion uint.

TypeScript encode/decode functions: encodeProofEnvelope(envelope: ProofEnvelope): Uint8Array and decodeProofEnvelope(bytes: Uint8Array): ProofEnvelope using a minimal CBOR library (cbor-x). Validates required fields and version range on decode.
TypeScript type definitions for ProofEnvelope, ProofType enum (human | agent | delegation), Groth16ProofPoints, and DelegationChainEntry.
Round-trip encode/decode tests for all three proof types, delegation chain presence/absence, version mismatch rejection, and malformed CBOR rejection.

Conformance test vectors: hex-encoded CBOR bytes paired with canonical JSON representations for human proof, agent proof, delegation proof, and a chained delegation. Used by any implementation to verify interop.
Integration guide: how to attach application/bolyra-proof+cbor as a Content-Type header, consume the envelope in a verifier, and extend with custom fields using the reserved CBOR map key range.

steps: Survey existing serialization usage across sdk/src, integrations/, and spec/ to identify fields already in use (proof bytes layout, public signals ordering, nonce placement) so the envelope schema captures current reality.

Define the CDDL schema in spec/proof-envelope.cddl: top-level map with integer keys (0=protocolVersion uint, 1=proofType uint enum, 2=publicSignals [+ bstr], 3=proof {a: [bstr,bstr], b: [[bstr,bstr],[bstr,bstr]], c: [bstr,bstr]}, 4=delegationChain [* DelegationEntry], 5=sessionNonce bstr ?); reserve keys 16–31 for private extensions.

Write spec/proof-envelope.md covering: COSE alignment rationale (why envelope-not-Sign1), media type string (application/bolyra-proof+cbor), IANA registration template (RFC 6838 §4.2), canonicalization rules (definite-length encoding, sorted integer keys), versioning policy (protocolVersion=1 is this spec, decode MUST reject unknown major versions).
Add cbor-x as a dependency in sdk/package.json (runtime dep, ~6 kB minified); audit for supply-chain risk and pin to exact version in overrides if a transitive advisory exists.
Implement sdk/src/types/envelope.ts with ProofType enum, Groth16ProofPoints interface, DelegationChainEntry interface, and ProofEnvelope interface; export from sdk/src/index.ts.

Implement sdk/src/envelope.ts: encodeProofEnvelope validates all required fields, serializes to CBOR map with integer keys, returns Uint8Array; decodeProofEnvelope parses CBOR, validates protocolVersion range, checks required keys, throws typed errors (EnvelopeDecodeError subclassing BolyraError).

Generate conformance test vectors by running encodeProofEnvelope on known fixture inputs (reuse existing sdk/test fixtures for proof points and public signals), then hex-encode and store in spec/conformance/proof-envelope-vectors.json alongside the canonical JSON representation.

Write sdk/test/envelope.test.ts covering: round-trip for human/agent/delegation types, delegation chain with two entries, encode→decode identity, decoding a vector from the conformance file, rejection of future major version (protocolVersion=255), rejection of truncated CBOR, rejection of missing required key.
Run npm run test:circuits:fast and npm run test:contracts to confirm no regressions from the new sdk dependency.
Update sdk/QUICKSTART.md and integrations/mcp/README.md to reference the new media type and show a minimal encode/decode snippet.
Commit all artifacts with DCO sign-off (git commit -s); include a note in CHANGELOG.md under [Unreleased] for the new proof-envelope module and media type.
dependencies: cbor-x (or equivalent deterministic CBOR library) as a new sdk/ runtime dependency
RFC 6838 media type registration template (informational — no blocking external process for pre-standard use)
RFC 9052 COSE structure patterns (informational reference for envelope design)
Existing sdk/src/index.ts public API surface (encode/decode must be additive exports)
Existing proof point byte layout from circuits/build .zkey artifacts (defines bstr lengths in the CDDL)
spec/draft-bolyra-mutual-zkp-auth-01.md (must cross-reference the new envelope spec)
